description: Two related problems: (1) FeatureData in state.ts is a flat monolithic bag that grows with every new layer — doesn't scale. (2) No documented path for raw→processed data format, so adding a new dataset requires reading existing code to guess what format is expected. Deliverables: (A) Define canonical processed data format per layer type — terrain (.f32 binary → TerrainProvider), imagery (XYZ/TMS tiles → ImageryLayer), vector lines (GeoJSON MultiLineString → GroundPolyline), points (GeoJSON FeatureCollection → Billboard/Label/Primitive). (B) Refactor FeatureData so each layer declares its own data dependency rather than a shared bag — e.g. each layer's init() fetches its own data, or data is keyed by layer ID. (C) Write scripts/README.md documenting the raw→processed playbook per type: what tool to use, what output format, where the output goes in data/processed/. This spike should run alongside RP-4 (arch refactor) and inform it — the layer interface design and data loading strategy are coupled.
title: Spike: data pipeline conventions + FeatureData refactor
